title: A crew member needs a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## To work in Canada as a crew member
FIFA-invited workers and volunteers
You don’t need a work permit to work in Canada if you’ve been directly invited to work at the FIFA World Cup 26™.
You may need to apply for a visitor visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) .
You may be a truck driver, a bus driver, or a shipping, maritime vessel or airline worker.
If this describes you, you don’t need a work permit if
- you’re working on foreign-owned and registered vehicles that are used mainly to transport cargo and passengers internationally, and
- your work is related to operating vehicles or serving passengers

## If you don’t meet these conditions
You need a work permit.
Find out if you’re eligible for a work permit (opens in a new tab) .

### If you’re working on a maritime vessel
You need a work permit if you don’t meet the conditions outlined above. There are some things your employer must do before you apply for your work permit to work on a maritime vessel.
Learn more about working on a maritime vessel.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
